product_overview: Resilience Graph AI is an AI-augmented SOC command center that turns raw security logs into a correlated incident story, ATT&CK attack chain, predicted next moves, actor attribution, and gated response guidance. It supports both pre-cached demo scenarios and live analysis of uploaded or API-submitted event data.
core_goals: Detect low-and-slow attacks from real infrastructure logs using anomaly detection.
Correlate per-event signals into a single explainable incident.
Map incident activity to MITRE ATT&CK techniques and an attack-path graph.
Predict the attacker’s next technique(s) from a partial chain.
Provide transparent actor attribution and threat-intel enrichment.
Generate audit-ready incident reports and evaluation metrics for SOC review.
Support live analysis via scenario selection, raw event arrays, and CSV upload.

features.name: List shipped demo scenarios
features.description: Allows users to view the prebuilt scenarios that can be analyzed in the demo app.
user_flows: GET /api/scenarios -> Receive 200 with a list of shipped demo scenarios including names, labels, and event counts
GET /api/scenarios -> Receive 200 with scenario metadata that can be used to choose a live analysis target

features.name: Analyze a named scenario
features.description: Runs the full analysis pipeline on one of the shipped scenarios and returns the complete incident bundle.
user_flows: POST /api/analyze with a valid scenario name -> Receive 200 with overview, incident, graph, threat intel, report, attackers, and meta bundle
POST /api/analyze with an unknown scenario name -> Receive 404 indicating the scenario is not recognized
POST /api/analyze with no scenario and no events payload -> Receive 422 validation error because analysis input is missing

features.name: Analyze raw events
features.description: Processes a user-provided array of events in the common schema and generates the full incident analysis.
user_flows: POST /api/analyze with a valid events array -> Receive 200 with correlated incident outputs, attack graph, attribution, and report
POST /api/analyze with a valid events array and account scoping -> Receive 200 with scoped incident analysis for the specified account
POST /api/analyze with an empty events array -> Receive 422 validation error for empty input
POST /api/analyze with more than 50k event rows -> Receive 422 validation error for oversized input
POST /api/analyze with an unknown account identifier -> Receive 422 validation error for invalid account scope

features.name: Analyze uploaded CSV logs
features.description: Accepts a multipart CSV upload, normalizes timestamp formats, and runs the live analysis pipeline on the uploaded event log.
user_flows: POST /api/analyze/upload with a valid CSV file -> Receive 200 with live analysis results for the uploaded log
POST /api/analyze/upload with CSV rows using epoch-integer timestamps -> Receive 200 after timestamps are normalized and analyzed
POST /api/analyze/upload with CSV rows using ISO-8601 timestamps -> Receive 200 after timestamps are normalized and analyzed
POST /api/analyze/upload with a malformed or unsupported upload -> Receive 422 validation error

features.name: Score a single event
features.description: Scores one authentication event using the behavioral anomaly model and returns anomaly severity for fast triage.
user_flows: POST /api/score-event with a valid auth event -> Receive 200 with anomaly_score and severity
POST /api/score-event with an event missing required behavioral fields -> Receive 422 validation error

features.name: Predict next ATT&CK technique(s)
features.description: Predicts the attacker’s next likely technique(s) from a partial technique chain using the shipped sequence model.
user_flows: POST /api/predict-next with a partial ATT&CK chain -> Receive 200 with next-technique predictions and ranking
POST /api/predict-next with an empty or invalid chain payload -> Receive 422 validation error

features.name: Threat radar enrichment and scoring
features.description: Surfaces external threat-intel items and scores them against the current incident using technique, actor, and edge cross-references.
user_flows: GET /api/threat-radar -> Receive 200 with cached radar items and metadata
POST /api/threat-radar with incident context -> Receive 200 with scored radar items cross-referenced to the incident
POST /api/threat-radar requesting live refresh -> Receive 200 with refreshed items when feeds respond or cached data when they do not
POST /api/threat-radar with insufficient incident context -> Receive 422 validation error

routes.description: Health check; returns ok and whether the cache is built
Cached Overview payload (MTTD, active incident, scorecard)
Cached attack-path graph (nodes, edges, pivots, crown jewels)
List of shipped demo scenarios with name, label, n_events, critical_default
POST — run the full live analysis on a scenario or an events array; optional account scoping and critical_assets
POST multipart — analyze an uploaded CSV event log
GET SSE — stream per-event scores for a scenario, then a done event with the full bundle
POST — score one auth event's behavioral features with the IsolationForest
POST — predict the next ATT&CK technique(s) from a partial chain (Markov)
GET cached radar / POST scored radar cross-referenced with the incident; optional live refresh

features.name: Analyze a scenario
features.description: Run the whole spine on a named scenario; returns overview/incident/graph/threat_intel/report/attackers/meta

features.name: Analyze raw events
features.description: Analyze an events array (rows in the common schema); optional account and critical_assets

features.name: Analyze uploaded CSV
features.description: Analyze a multipart CSV upload; must handle epoch-int and ISO-8601 timestamps

features.name: Score an event
features.description: Score behavioral features; returns anomaly_score and severity

features.name: Predict next technique
features.description: Predict next ATT&CK technique(s) from a partial chain

features.name: Threat radar scored
features.description: Radar items scored against an incident (technique_ids, actors, edges); optional live refresh

known_limitations.issue: analyze rejects oversized (>50k rows) and empty inputs with HTTP 422
analyze requires either scenario or events; unknown scenario returns 404, unknown account returns 422
threat-radar live refresh falls back to cache if no feed responds
known_limitations.impact: Returns cached data with meta.source=cache, never an empty live result
